title: Increased chance of Prion Disease (PrP 129 Met homozygote)
summary: Increased chance of Prion Disease (PrP 129 Met homozygote)
detail: This genotype encodes for a homozygous Methionine at position 129 of PRNP, the Prion Protein gene (PrP-129MM). Unlike the protective M/V heterozgotes and V homozygous, homozygosity at this residue is associated with an increased susceptibility to sporadic CJD and and confers susceptibility against vCJD. All reported cases of vCJD, (linked to ingestion of 'mad cow disease' contaminated meat) have been 129 Met homozygous
